module.name: Space Force Command
module.description: USSF-ready progression aligned with AFDP 3-14, SDP 3-0, and the Guardian Ideal across space operations, cyber defense, intelligence fusion, leadership, and resilience pillars.

space_force_cyber_defense.name: Cyber Defense
space_force_cyber_defense.description: SDP 3-0 cyber protection teaming focused on MDT network hardening, incident response, and Zero-Trust enforcement for space mission systems.

space_force_fleet_coordination_protocols.name: Fleet Coordination Protocols
space_force_fleet_coordination_protocols.description: Synchronization drills for comms discipline, formation integrity, and adaptive routing when dozens of hulls share the same battlespace.

space_force_intelligence_and_analysis.name: Intelligence & Analysis
space_force_intelligence_and_analysis.description: AFDP 2-0 and AFDP 3-14 grounded space ISR fusion, JADC2 integration, and coalition intel-sharing drills for Delta S2/G2 leads.
focus: Intelligence Preparation of the Operational Environment

space_force_joint_space_operations.name: Joint Space Operations
space_force_joint_space_operations.description: Command-and-control integration with Air Force, Space Operations Command, and allied partners covering network-centric comms, link management, and mission rehearsal.

space_force_leadership_and_professionali.name: Leadership & Professionalism
space_force_leadership_and_professionali.description: Guardian Ideal and OTS curriculum focused on emotional intelligence, ethical command climate, and Just Culture decision framing for flight commanders.

space_force_microgravity_readiness.name: Microgravity Readiness
space_force_microgravity_readiness.description: Physiological conditioning, vestibular adaptation, and restraint/suit procedures that keep USSF crews effective during extended microgravity operations.

space_force_nebula_siege_resilience.name: Nebula Siege Resilience
space_force_nebula_siege_resilience.description: Psychophysiological hardening for long-haul sieges: radiation discipline, systems triage, and morale leadership when sensors go blind.

space_force_orbital_operations_planning.name: Orbital Operations Planning
space_force_orbital_operations_planning.description: Mission design fundamentals for real-world USSF guardians: orbital mechanics, conjunction assessment, and rapid response planning for contested space.

space_force_orbital_tactics_command.name: Orbital Tactics Command
space_force_orbital_tactics_command.description: Mission-planning doctrine for multi-vector strikes, debris-field maneuvering, and synchronized drop-pod deployment across contested orbits.

space_force_physical_and_resilience.name: Physical & Resilience
space_force_physical_and_resilience.description: USSF Physical Training Guide plus Guardian Resilience Framework combining aerobic standards with cognitive resilience drills for operational crews.

space_force_space_contingency_resilience.name: Space Contingency Resilience
space_force_space_contingency_resilience.description: Mission assurance drills for anomaly response, radiation weather events, and sustaining morale/communications during prolonged space contingencies.

space_force_space_operations.name: Space Operations
space_force_space_operations.description: AFDP 3-14 aligned mastery of orbital mechanics, payload employment, and SDA tasking for Delta crews.

space_force_zero_gravity_conditioning.name: Zero-Gravity Conditioning
space_force_zero_gravity_conditioning.description: Core stabilization, vestibular resilience, and microgravity joint-loading drills that keep operators lethal when the deck disappears.

space_force_cyber_defense_cyber_threat_r.name: Cyber Threat Response
space_force_cyber_defense_cyber_threat_r.description: Mission Defense Team battle drills for live incidents.

cards.title: MDT Battle Drill 0
cards.description: Practice the initial triage call: confirm indicators, halt spread, and notify C3 with mission impact statements per USSF MDT CONOPS. Ref: SDP 3-0.
bulletpoints: Capture who/what/when — this concept is critical for effective mdt battle drill 0 because practitioners must understand how it integrates with related operational procedures and real-world application contexts

Stabilize critical services — this concept is critical for effective mdt battle drill 0 because practitioners must understand how it integrates with related operational procedures and real-world application contexts

Notify battle staff — this concept is critical for effective mdt battle drill 0 because practitioners must understand how it integrates with related operational procedures and real-world application contexts

Advanced practitioners demonstrate competency through space debris mitigation, applying theoretical knowledge to novel situations and adapting standard procedures to meet the specific demands of each unique operational context encountered

Key areas include Capture who/what/when — this concept is critical for effective mdt battle drill 0 because practitioners must understand how it...

exercises.prompt: From memory, list the key points of "MDT Battle Drill 0".
exercises.expectedOutcome: A comprehensive explanation of the core principles of mdt battle drill 0, demonstrating clear understanding of key concepts, their interrelationships, and practical significance in space operations, satellite systems, and orbital mechanics.

exercises.prompt: Why is mdt battle drill 0 important? What happens if these principles are ignored?
hints: Consider the consequences of inaction.
Think about how this connects to adjacent topics.
exercises.expectedOutcome: Understanding the importance and interconnections of mdt battle drill 0.

exercises.prompt: Design a step-by-step procedure for applying mdt battle drill 0 in a field scenario involving orbital analysis. Include specific decision points and contingency actions.
exercises.expectedOutcome: A structured procedure with at least four steps, each including a specific action, the rationale behind it, and contingency options if conditions change during execution in the context of space operations, satellite systems, and orbital mechanics.
hints: Begin with the initial assessment phase.
Include decision criteria at each major step.
Address what to do when standard procedures are insufficient.

exercises.prompt: You are a space operations officer monitoring a potential anti-satellite threat. You must decide how to handle a situation involving mdt battle drill 0. Which approach would be most effective for addressing mdt battle drill in this context?
choices: Immediately deploy mdt battle drill protocols without further assessment
Conduct a thorough assessment of the situation, then apply mdt battle drill principles systematically while monitoring for indicators of capture who/what/when
Delay all action until receiving explicit authorization from higher authority
Apply standard procedures without considering the specific capture who/what/when factors present
exercises.expectedOutcome: The best approach combines careful assessment with systematic application. In space operations, satellite systems, and orbital mechanics, impulsive action (option A) and rigid adherence to standard procedures without adaptation (option D) can be dangerous. Waiting for authorization (option C) may cause critical delays. Option B balances thoroughness with action, incorporating awareness of capture who/what/when while applying mdt battle drill principles.
hints: Consider the immediate risks and potential consequences of each option.
Think about what information you need before acting.

learningObjectives: Describe the core principles of mdt battle drill 0
Apply knowledge of capture who/what/when
Evaluate the significance of practice the initial triage call: confirm indicators, halt spread, and notify c3 with mission impact statements per ussf mdt conops

bulletpoints: Select indicators — this concept is critical for effective indications & warnings matrix because practitioners must understand how it integrates with related operational procedures and real-world application contexts

Assign thresholds — this concept is critical for effective indications & warnings matrix because practitioners must understand how it integrates with related operational procedures and real-world application contexts

Pre-brief actions — this concept is critical for effective indications & warnings matrix because practitioners must understand how it integrates with related operational procedures and real-world application contexts

Advanced practitioners demonstrate competency through space debris mitigation, applying theoretical knowledge to novel situations and adapting standard procedures to meet the specific demands of each unique operational context encountered
cards.summaryText: Populate an I&W matrix that links indicators, thresholds, and associated collection plans for space threats. Key areas include Select indicators — this concept is critical for effective indications & warnings matrix because practitioners must understand how it integrates with ...

exercises.prompt: From memory, list the key points of "Indications & Warnings Matrix".
exercises.expectedOutcome: A comprehensive explanation of the core principles of indications & warnings matrix, demonstrating clear understanding of key concepts, their interrelationships, and practical significance in space operations, satellite systems, and orbital mechanics.

exercises.prompt: Why is indications & warnings matrix important? What happens if these principles are ignored?
hints: Consider the consequences of inaction.
Think about how this connects to adjacent topics.
exercises.expectedOutcome: Understanding the importance and interconnections of indications & warnings matrix.

exercises.prompt: Design a step-by-step procedure for applying indications & warnings matrix in a field scenario involving orbital analysis. Include specific decision points and contingency actions.
exercises.expectedOutcome: A structured procedure with at least four steps, each including a specific action, the rationale behind it, and contingency options if conditions change during execution in the context of space operations, satellite systems, and orbital mechanics.
hints: Begin with the initial assessment phase.
Include decision criteria at each major step.
Address what to do when standard procedures are insufficient.

exercises.prompt: You are a space operations officer monitoring a potential anti-satellite threat. You must decide how to handle a situation involving indications & warnings matrix. Which approach would be most effective for addressing indications in this context?
choices: Immediately deploy indications protocols without further assessment
Conduct a thorough assessment of the situation, then apply indications principles systematically while monitoring for indicators of warnings matrix
Delay all action until receiving explicit authorization from higher authority
Apply standard procedures without considering the specific warnings matrix factors present
exercises.expectedOutcome: The best approach combines careful assessment with systematic application. In space operations, satellite systems, and orbital mechanics, impulsive action (option A) and rigid adherence to standard procedures without adaptation (option D) can be dangerous. Waiting for authorization (option C) may cause critical delays. Option B balances thoroughness with action, incorporating awareness of warnings matrix while applying indications principles.
hints: Consider the immediate risks and potential consequences of each option.
Think about what information you need before acting.

learningObjectives: Describe the core principles of indications & warnings matrix
Apply knowledge of select indicators
Evaluate the significance of populate an i&w matrix that links indicators, thresholds, and associated collection plans for space threats
